auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants (Mike Chang, CPA, CA – Port Coquitlam)
one_time_description: Comprehensive land claim negotiation loan from Government of Canada forgiven in the year; Kitasoo's portion of $36,238,158 forgiveness was $5,020,768, previously recorded as long-term debt now recognized as revenue
economic_notes: Entities accounted for by modified equity method include Kitasoo Seafoods Ltd., Kitasoo Development Corporation, Kitasoo Hydro Power Ltd., Kitasoo Forest Company Ltd., Klemtu Tourism Ltd., Kitasoo Band Store Ltd., Kitasoo Development GP Ltd., Kitasoo Aqua Farm Ltd. Significant new funding: $1,840,000 from Coast Funds and $500,000 from Province of BC for hydro penstock expansion; $573,400 from BCFN Gaming Revenue Sharing. Joint venture interest in Western Princess vessel (50%). COVID-19 noted as subsequent event.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020 (March 31, 2020). Auditor firm name not explicitly stated as a firm name — partners listed under a Port Coquitlam CPA practice; engagement partner is Mike Chang CPA CA. Operating loan of $2,765,688 exists as a current liability. Silviculture liability of $1,052,449 included in liabilities. Intangible assets of $2,022,000 (fishing, sea cucumber, forestry licenses) are indefinite-life and not amortized.
